meta.title: Kocuria polaris sp. nov., an orange-pigmented psychrophilic bacterium isolated from an Antarctic cyanobacterial mat sample
meta.journalTitle: International Journal of Systematic and Evolutionary Microbiology

abstractHtml: Strain CMS 76orT, an orange-pigmented bacterium, was isolated from a cyanobacterial mat sample from a pond located in McMurdo Dry Valley, Antarctica. On the basis of chemotaxonomic and phylogenetic properties, strain CMS 76orT was identified as a member of the genus Kocuria. It exhibited a 16S rDNA similarity of 99·8 % and DNADNA similarity of 71 % with Kocuria rosea (ATCC 186T). Phenotypic traits confirmed that strain CMS 76orT and K. rosea were well differentiated. Furthermore, strain CMS 76orT could be differentiated from the other reported species of Kocuria, namely Kocuria kristinae (ATCC 27570T), Kocuria varians (ATCC 15306T), Kocuria rhizophila (DSM 11926T) and Kocuria palustris (DSM 11025T), on the basis of a number of phenotypic features. Therefore, it is proposed that strain CMS 76orT (=MTCC 3702T =DSM 14382T) be assigned to a novel species of the genus Kocuria, as Kocuria polaris.
